segments.text: A prophecy: The word of the Lord to Israel through Malachi.
I have loved you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But you ask,
How have you loved us?
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Was not Esau Jacob's brother?
Yet I have loved Jacob,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Esau I have hated, and I have turned his hill country into a wasteland and left his inheritance to the desert jackals.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Though we have been crushed, we will rebuild the ruins.
segments.speaker: Edom

segments.text: But this is what the Lord Almighty says:
They may build, but I will demolish. They will be called the Wicked Land, a people always under the wrath of the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: You will see it with your own eyes and say,
Great is the Lord —even beyond the borders of Israel!
segments.speaker: Unspecified

segments.text: A son honors his father, and a slave his master. If I am a father, where is the honor due me? If I am a master, where is the respect due me? It is you priests who show contempt for my name.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: But you ask,
How have we shown contempt for your name?
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: By offering defiled food on my altar.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But you ask,
How have we defiled you?
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: By saying that the Lord's table is contemptible.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When you offer blind animals for sacrifice, is that not wrong? When you sacrifice lame or diseased animals, is that not wrong? Try offering them to your governor! Would he be pleased with you? Would he accept you?
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Now plead with God to be gracious to us. With such offerings from your hands, will he accept you?
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Oh, that one of you would shut the temple doors, so that you would not light useless fires on my altar! I am not pleased with you, and I will accept no offering from your hands.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: My name will be great among the nations, from where the sun rises to where it sets. In every place incense and pure offerings will be brought to me, because my name will be great among the nations.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: But you profane it by saying, 'The Lord's table is defiled,' and, 'Its food is contemptible.'
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: And you say, 'What a burden!' and you sniff at it contemptuously. When you bring injured, lame or diseased animals and offer them as sacrifices, should I accept them from your hands?
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Cursed is the cheat who has an acceptable male in his flock and vows to give it, but then sacrifices a blemished animal to the Lord. For I am a great king, and my name is to be feared among the nations.
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty
